exits.label: ◂ back toward the last torch and the lit causeway

npcs.name: Symon
npcs.title: a soul that will not stop running
npcs.hail: *(Far out at the edge of the dark, where the causeway's light gives way to the long nothing, a soul is running — has been running, you sense, for years, a young man with his coat half-off one shoulder and his face turned always backward over it, watching for the thing behind him. He does not run *toward* anywhere. He runs *from.* And when your cold reaches him across the dark he flinches like a struck deer and his whole body coils to bolt, because to Symon every shape that approaches is the shape come at last to catch him, and being caught — he is certain, certain as the cold in your chest — means being *judged*, and being judged means being *named*, and the name he runs from is the one he cannot survive hearing said aloud.)*

npcs.name: Gethin
npcs.title: a soul frozen into the hunt
npcs.hail: *(Between you and the running man paces a third soul, and this one does not run and does not tire — it *hunts*, low and tireless and baying, a broad grey shade that was a man once and is now very nearly only a *purpose.* Gethin was a thief-taker in life, a hunter of fugitives, the best on the Coast — and when he died with a hunt unfinished his soul caught on the nearest fleeing thing the way a burr catches wool, and he has run Symon to ground a thousand times and let him up a thousand times, because the *catching* was never the point to a soul like this; the *hunt* is. He has forgotten the man he chases. He has forgotten his own dead name. He remembers only the shape of the chase, and runs it, and runs it, because it is the last thing of himself he still holds.)*

npcs.name: Edrid
npcs.title: the one who chases to forgive
npcs.hail: *(Trailing far behind the hunt, neither baying like the hound nor bolting like the quarry, comes a fourth soul — a slight young shade, younger than the runner, walking where the others run, tired in the way only the patient grow tired. This is Edrid, and the edges of him still carry the faint char of the fire that killed him, and he has followed his brother across the whole dark country of the dead not to catch him — he could never catch him, he doesn't run — but in the hope that someday Symon will simply *stop*, and turn, and let Edrid say the one thing he has carried unspoken since the night the roof came down: a thing Symon has spent years running from precisely because he believes it must be a curse, when it is the opposite, and is the only thing in any world that could ever let him rest.)*

variants.text: *(He does not let you get close. The moment your cold falls near him he's three paces gone, light on his feet, coat flaring, already half-turned to run — and he speaks fast, breathless, the words of a man who has had this conversation in his head ten thousand times and never once out loud.)* …*No.* *No, no, don't* — don't *come* at me, I *know* what you are, you're — you're *another* one, sent to *bring* me *in*, to *hold* me still so the *Hound* can — *(he edges back)* — I'm *not* *stopping.* You *hear* me? I *stopped* once. *One* time. *Years* back, I was so *tired* I just — I *sat down* in the dark and *let* it come, and the *Hound* got its teeth in me and started *dragging* me *back* toward the *light*, toward the *naming*, the *judging*, and I *clawed* free and I *swore* — *never* *again.* *(his eyes are wild)* You don't *understand.* If they *catch* me — if they *make* me *stop* and *say* my *name* and *weigh* what I *did* — there's only *one* verdict, there was *always* only *one*, I *earned* it the night I — *(he chokes it off)* — *no.* *No, I'm* not *saying* it. Saying it makes it *true.* Running keeps it — keeps it *not-yet.* So I *run.* That's all there *is* now. The *running.* It's the only thing between me and the *worst* thing I know about myself. *(he tenses)* …Don't. Don't *reach* for me. I *will* *bolt.* I *always* bolt.

nodes.speaker: Symon
nodes.text: *(He hovers at the edge of bolting, weight on his back foot, watching your hands — but he hasn't run yet, and that, you sense, is more than most get from him. He is so tired. Under the terror, he is so unspeakably tired of running.)* …*Say* it *quick*, then. *(he swallows)* Whatever you came to say. But I'm *warning* you — one *wrong* word, one *step*, one *reach* for my *arm* — and I'm *gone*, and you'll *not* see me again. *(barely)* Make it *worth* the standing still. I don't *do* it often.

choices.text: *(read what he's truly running from — gently, without moving)* "Stay still a moment, Symon. I only want to look. I won't take a step."
"I'm not the Hound, Symon, and I'm not its handler. I'm not here to hold you for anyone. Look at me — no chain, no writ. Just a soul, cold like you."

*(the one who knows a soul isn't its worst moment)* "Symon. Listen, because I died and came back and I learned the one thing that can stop a soul running: you're not running from the Hound. You never were. You're running from a *name* — 'coward' — that you pinned on yourself for the worst minute of your life. But a soul is not its worst minute. You were a frightened boy in a fire for *one* breath, and you've sentenced your whole self to run forever for it. Stop. The thing chasing you is a verdict you're carrying. You can set it down. I'll help you carry it instead — but you have to stop running long enough to hand it over."

*(don't grab — root him; say the one true thing that makes a fleeing soul choose, for once, to stay)* "Symon — one question, then you can run all you like. In all these years of running… has it ever once gotten you *away* from it? Or do you carry it at exactly your own speed, no matter how fast you go?"
"The one chasing you behind the Hound — the young one, Edrid — he isn't hunting you to punish you, Symon. He's been trying to catch you to *forgive* you. That's why you can't outrun him."
"Go on and run, then, Symon. I'll not chase. But I'll be here when you've a mind to stop."

nodes.speaker: Symon
nodes.text: *(You hold still as stone and let your cold read him, and the reading is not what the running suggests. There is no real flight in Symon — there is nowhere he is trying to reach. There is only a single night he cannot get away from, and a name he cannot bear to be called.)* *(Your sense reads it whole: Symon was nineteen, and his little brother Edrid was twelve, and the house took fire in the black of night, and Symon — closest to the door, fastest on his feet, the one their dying mother had charged with the boy's keeping — Symon *froze*, and then Symon *ran*, out the door and into the cold yard, and stood there in his nightshirt watching the windows go gold, and did not go back, *could* not make his legs go back, while his brother screamed his name from the upper room. Edrid died in the fire. Symon lived, and has been running from that yard ever since, in life and now in death, because the moment he stops is the moment he is again the boy who stood in the grass and did not go back. He does not flee the Hound. He flees the word his own mouth will not say: not 'coward' — worse — 'I heard him calling and I did not go.')*

nodes.speaker: Symon
nodes.text: *(He stares at your empty hands a long moment, chest heaving though the dead need no breath, and some fraction of the coil goes out of his legs — not trust, not yet, but the exhausted flicker of a creature that has wanted, for years, to be told exactly this.)* …*Not* the Hound. *(warily)* …*Everyone's* the Hound, to me. *(he doesn't run, though)* You — you really *aren't*, are you. There's no — no *chain* on you, no *order.* *(a shaky breath)* Do you know what it's *like*, cold one, to spend *years* certain that every soul who comes near is coming to *drag* you to the *worst* thing about yourself? You stop being able to tell a *rescuer* from a *gaoler.* They *blur.* *(he wipes his face)* …But you're just — *standing* there. *Letting* me run if I want. *(barely)* *Nobody* lets me run. The Hound *bays* me on, the young one *follows*, everyone *wants* something from my *stopping.* *(quietly, the most dangerous thing he could admit)* …You're the first in a *long* while that I'm not, this *second*, running from. *(he catches himself, alarmed at it)* …Don't make me *regret* that. *Please.* I have *so* little left to *risk*, and *standing here* with you is *all* of it.

nodes.speaker: Symon
nodes.text: *(He goes utterly still — and it is a different stillness than the coiled, about-to-bolt kind; it is the stillness of a running animal that has heard, for the first time, a sound that is not a predator.)* …*Not* my *worst* minute. *(barely)* …You — you *know.* Don't you. What I — *(he can't finish, and for once he doesn't run from the not-finishing)* …I've been *running* so *long*, cold one, that I *forgot* there was a *me* doing the running. I thought I *was* the running. I thought if I *stopped* I'd just — *be* the *boy* in the *yard*, *forever*, frozen, *useless*, with my brother's voice in the — *(he shudders)* — but you're saying the boy in the yard was *one* *breath.* *One.* And I've made him the *whole* of me. *(a sound that is almost a sob)* …*Nineteen.* I was *nineteen*, and the *fire* was *between* me and the *stair*, and my *legs* — my *legs* just — *(he stops, and stays stopped, which is the miracle)* …You'd *carry* it. The verdict. With me. *(he looks at your cold hands, at the lack of a chain)* I don't — I don't even know how to *stop*, cold one. The running's so *old.* But you're the first to tell me the thing I run from *fits in a hand* — that it's a *weight* I can *set down*, and not the *entire* *shape* of my *soul.* *(barely, trembling, rooted)* …*Show* me. *Show* me how a soul that did the *worst* thing sets it *down* and is still *allowed* to be a *soul.* I'll — I'll *stand* here. I'll *try* to stand here. *Gods* help me, I'll *try.*

nodes.speaker: Symon
nodes.text: *(You don't move a muscle toward him. You just ask it — has the running ever once gotten you away from it? — in a voice so level, so free of judgment, so plainly *curious* about the true answer, that the question slips under years of terror and lands in the one place still capable of hearing it. And Symon stops. Not edges-back stops. *Stops.* The coat settles on his shoulders. His back foot comes flat to the ground for the first time in years.)* …*No.* *(the word falls out of him, wondering and wrecked)* …*No.* It hasn't. *(he looks down at his own legs as if seeing them new)* I've run — gods, I've run *across the whole dead world*, cold one, faster and farther than any soul ever ran, and the *yard* is — the *yard* is *right* *here*, it's *always* *been* right here, the *exact* same distance, *no* matter how — *(he laughs, a terrible cracked sound)* — I never *outran* it *once.* Not a *single* *step.* I've been running *in place* for *years*, carrying the fire *with* me, *certain* the running was *working.* *(he sinks down — actually sits, in the dark, the way he hasn't dared since the time the Hound took him)* …It was *never* going to *work*, was it. You can't *outrun* a thing you carry *inside* the *running.* *(he looks up at you, undone, still)* …*Oh.* I'm *tired*, cold one. I'm so — I've been tired for *years* and I never let myself *feel* it because *feeling* it meant *stopping.* *(barely)* I've stopped. Look. I've *stopped.* *Now* what do I do? *Now* that I'm not *running* — *what's* a soul that did what I did, *for?*

nodes.speaker: Symon
nodes.text: *(You hold still and ask it plainly — has the running ever gotten you away from it? — and the question reaches him, you can see it land, a crack of doubt in the long certainty of flight. He doesn't sit. But he doesn't bolt, either. He *wavers*, caught between two whole years of habit and one true question.)* …*Away* from it. *(slowly)* …I — *(his weight shifts forward, back, forward)* …I *tell* myself it does. That if I just go *fast* enough, *far* enough — *(he falters)* — but I'm *at* the *edge* of the *dark*, cold one. There's *nowhere* *farther.* I've *been* running *out* of *world* for a *while* now. *(he glances over his shoulder at the nothing beyond the causeway)* And it's — it's *still* with me. The *yard.* The *gold* in the *windows.* It *keeps* my *pace* *exactly.* *(troubled, hovering)* …You've put a *thorn* in it. The running. I can *feel* it now, the — the *uselessness* of it, where before there was just the *need.* *(he steadies, doesn't flee)* …I can't *stop.* Not *yet.* It's too *old*, the running, my *legs* don't *know* how to *not.* But you've — you've made me *doubt* it, and I haven't *doubted* the running in *years.* *(barely)* *Stay.* Ask me *again*, another way. I think — I think if you ask me *enough* true things, I might *forget* to *run* long enough to *learn* how to *stand.*

nodes.speaker: Symon
nodes.text: *(You ask the question — but it comes a half-beat too eager, with a half-step of your weight behind it, and to a soul who has survived years by reading exactly that, the eagerness reads as a *grab.* His eyes snap wide.)* …You're — you're *working* me. *(he's already moving)* That's a — that's a *catcher's* question, that's the *kind* thing they say right before the *hand* closes, I *know* it, I've *heard* it — *(he's three paces gone, then five)* — *no.* *No*, I *almost* — I *nearly* *stood* there, and that's *exactly* when they *get* you, when you *nearly* — *(backing into the dark)* — *don't* *follow.* *(barely, over his shoulder)* …It *was* a good question, cold one. That's what *frightens* me. *Come* *back* when you can *ask* it without *wanting* my *stopping* so *badly* I can *smell* it. I run from *want.* I always have. *Wanting* is what the *fire* did — it *wanted* me to go *back* in and I *couldn't* — so *don't* *want* anything *from* me. Just — *be* there. *Not* *wanting.* That's the *only* thing I can *stand* near.

nodes.speaker: Symon
nodes.text: *(In your care to root him you reach — just slightly, just an instinctive half-inch of your cold hand toward his arm, the oldest helping gesture there is — and it is the one thing in all the worlds Symon cannot survive. The fire wanted him to reach. He has spent his death not-reaching. Your hand moves and he is simply *gone*, a streak of coat into the dark, faster than grief.)* …*(His voice comes back from somewhere out in the nothing, thin and shaking and almost — almost — apologetic.)* …I *told* you. *(ragged)* I *told* you not to *reach.* *(a pause, the sound of a soul who hates its own flinch)* …It's not — it's not *you*, cold one. A *hand* coming at me is the — it's the *last* thing my *brother* *saw* me *not* do. Reach. *Into* the *fire.* For *him.* So when a hand reaches for *me* I — I *can't* — *(the voice frays)* …*Come* *back.* But *gods*, keep your *hands* where I can *see* them *still* and *open* and *not* *reaching.* I'll stand for *words.* I'll *never* stand for a *hand.* Not until I've forgiven the *one* I *didn't* *use.* *(fainter, fleeing)* …*Words* only. *Please.* *Words* I can *almost* *bear.*

nodes.speaker: Symon
nodes.text: *(For the first time he does not look ready to run. He looks like he has been struck somewhere very deep, and the stillness that comes over him is the stillness of a soul afraid that if it moves, it will shatter.)* …*Forgive* me. *(the word is barely sound)* …*Edrid* wants to — *(he shakes his head, fast)* — *no.* *No*, that's — that's *worse*, cold one, that's *worse* than the Hound, that's *worse* than *vengeance.* *(his voice climbs)* If he wanted to *hurt* me I could — I'd *understand* that, I've been *waiting* *years* for that, I *earned* that, the *hurting* makes *sense.* But *forgiveness* — *(he presses his hands to his face)* — *forgiveness* means I have to *look* at him. *See* his *face.* The face that was in the *upper* *window* calling my *name* while I stood in the *grass.* And if he *forgives* me, then I have to *forgive* — I have to let *myself* — *(he can't)* …*That's* why I run *hardest* from *him.* Isn't it. You're *right.* The *Hound* I can outrun forever, it only wants to *catch* me. But *Edrid* — Edrid wants to *free* me, and being *freed* means *facing* the *yard*, and I have *built* my whole *death* on never *facing* the *yard.* *(barely, trembling)* …Is he — is he really not *angry?* After what I — how is he *not* — *(a wrecked whisper)* …I can't. I *can't* let him forgive me, cold one. I don't *know* how to be a soul that's *been* *forgiven* for *that.* …Do *you?* Could you — could you *teach* me that, before you bring him? Because if he *says* it and I'm not *ready*, I'll *run*, and I'll *never* *stop*, and he'll have *carried* it all this way for *nothing.*

nodes.speaker: Symon
nodes.text: *(He blinks, plainly thrown — this, too, is something no one offers him.)* …You'll — you'll just *let* me. *(warily, edging but not bolting)* …*Be* here. When I want to stop. *(he doesn't know what to do with it)* …*Nobody* says that, cold one. They say *'stop running, Symon', 'face* it, *Symon', 'let* the *Hound* take you, it's for the *best.'* They *push.* And pushing makes me *run.* *(quietly)* …*You're* not pushing. *(he stares at you)* That's — that might be the only thing that's ever made me *want* to stop, in all these years: someone who'd *let* me not. *(barely, as he drifts back toward the dark, slower than usual)* …I'll — I'll circle back. I *do* that. I run, and I circle, and I come *near* again, when it's safe. *(a flicker of something almost like hope, quickly hidden)* …*Be* here when I circle. Don't *want* it too loud. And maybe — *(he's going, but looking back)* — maybe one of these circles I'll come near and *not* leave. I haven't *not-left* in *years.* But you — you might be worth the *standing still.* …We'll *see.* I always say *we'll see.* It's the closest a runner gets to a *promise.*

variants.text: *(The young shade stops when you approach — he does not startle like his brother, does not bay like the hound; he simply stops, and looks at you with the worn patience of a soul that has been walking a very long time and is in no hurry, because the thing he wants cannot be hurried.)* …You've *seen* him. *(it isn't a question; he reads it in your cold)* *Symon.* My brother. *(a tired, gentle smile that has char at its edges)* Still running. *Course* he is. *(he looks out toward the dark where the runner went)* I'm *Edrid.* I've been *following* him a *long* while now, cold one. Across — oh, across *all* of it. The whole dark country. *(quietly)* Everyone who sees us thinks they *know* the story. The *runner* and the one who *chases* him — they think I'm *vengeance.* The *wronged* little brother, *hounding* the *coward* who *let* him *burn.* *(he shakes his head, sad)* Even *Symon* thinks that. *Specially* Symon. It's *why* he runs from me *hardest* of all. *(he meets your eyes, and there is no anger in him anywhere, only a grief gone soft and patient with age)* But that's not — cold one, I'm not *chasing* him to *hurt* him. I could *never* catch him anyway; he's *always* been faster. I'm *following* him because I have *one thing* to *say*, and I have been trying to say it for the *whole* of both our deaths, and he will *not* *stop* *running* long enough to *hear* it. *(softly)* …Maybe *you* can make him stop. They say you're the kind that can.

nodes.speaker: Edrid
nodes.text: *(He settles, the way the truly patient settle, ready to wait as long as it takes.)* …*Ask*, cold one. *(a small smile)* I've had *years* of walking to think it all through. There's not much about my brother, or the fire, or what I'm carrying, that I haven't turned over a *thousand* times in the dark.

choices.text: "What's the one thing you've been trying to say to him, Edrid? You've chased him across the whole dead world to say it. What is it?"
"He thinks you're vengeance — that's exactly why he runs hardest from you. If he knew you weren't angry, would he stop? Or would the *forgiveness* frighten him more than the chase?"

*(the one who can carry what a fleeing soul won't stop to receive)* "Edrid — here's what I can do that your years of chasing can't. A soul running from forgiveness can outrun the one offering it forever — but it can't outrun a thing that's already *inside* the cold with it. Let me carry your forgiveness to Symon the way I carry everything: in the dead-touched cold he can't flee, spoken when he's finally still. You've been trying to hand it to him at a dead run. Let me hold it until he stops — and then he'll have to receive it, because it'll already be *in* him, not chasing behind."
"Tell me how it happened, Edrid. The fire. I'd know the truth of it from you, not just the shape Symon runs from."
"Walk a while longer, Edrid. I'll see if I can slow him for you."

nodes.speaker: Edrid
nodes.text: *(He is quiet a moment, and when he speaks it is with the simplicity of a thing rehearsed across years until all the heat has gone out of it and only the truth is left.)* …The thing I want to say. *(softly)* …It's *six* words, cold one. That's *all.* Six words I've carried *years* to deliver. *(he looks at the dark)* *'It wasn't your fault. I forgive you.'* *(he lets that sit)* …That's it. That's the *whole* of the terrible thing he's been running from. *(a sad smile)* He thinks I'm chasing him with a *knife.* I'm chasing him with *that.* *(quietly)* Because here's what Symon doesn't *know*, what he *ran* before he could ever *learn*: I was *twelve*, cold one, and I was *scared*, and I was *dying* in that upper room — but I was *also* his *little brother*, and I *knew* him, knew him *better* than anyone, and even *then*, even *calling* his name with the smoke coming up — I *understood.* He was *nineteen.* The *fire* was *between* him and the *stair.* His *legs* *wouldn't* *go*, the same way *anyone's* legs wouldn't, the same way *most* people's legs *don't* — and the only *unusual* thing my brother ever did was *survive* to *blame himself forever* for being *ordinarily, humanly afraid.* *(his voice catches, just once)* I forgave him *while it was happening*, cold one. I forgave him with the *last* breath I had. He just — he *ran* before he could *hear* it, and he's been running from a *curse* that was *never* a curse. It was always only ever *this.* *Six* words. And I will walk the dark *forever* to *give* them to him, because no little brother should let his big brother burn *himself* alive for *eighty* years over *one* night the fire was simply *stronger* than a *boy.*

nodes.speaker: Edrid
nodes.text: *(He nods slowly, the grief in him deepening with understanding.)* …You see it too. *(quietly)* …*Yes.* The *forgiveness* frightens him *more.* I worked that out *years* ago, cold one, and it nearly *broke* me, because it means the *very thing* I'm carrying to *free* him is the thing he runs *hardest* to *escape.* *(he looks toward the dark)* If I were *vengeance* — if I *hated* him — he could *bear* that. It would *fit.* It would *match* the verdict he's already passed on himself. A *hunted* coward is *still*, in his own eyes, a coward getting his *due.* *(softly)* But *forgiveness* doesn't *match.* Forgiveness says the verdict was *wrong* — that he was never the thing he's spent his death *being* — and to *accept* that, he'd have to *put down* the only self he's *known* since the fire, and *stand* in the yard *un-condemned*, and that terrifies him more than any *hound.* *(his voice softens)* He'd rather be *justly* *hunted* than *mercifully* *freed*, because the hunting *confirms* him and the *mercy* *unmakes* him. *(he meets your eyes)* That's the cruelty of what the fire did, cold one — it didn't just kill *me.* It taught my brother that he *deserves* to *suffer*, so *thoroughly* that he now *flees* the one thing that could *end* the suffering. *(barely)* …You'll have to make him *able* to be forgiven *before* you bring him my six words. Or they'll land on a soul that *can't hold* them, and he'll run, and I'll have to start *over.* I've started over *so* many times.

nodes.speaker: Edrid
nodes.text: *(Something lifts in him — the patient grief shot through, for the first time, with hope — and he reaches out and takes your cold hands in his char-edged ones, trusting, the way his brother never could.)* …You'd *carry* it. *(barely)* Into the cold where he *lives*, where he can't *outrun* it. *(his eyes fill)* …*Yes.* *Yes*, cold one — *take* it. *Take* the six words. *(he presses them into your hands as if they were a physical thing, warm)* I've been trying to *throw* them at a *running* man for *years*, and they *fall short*, *always*, because you *can't* *hand* a fleeing soul *anything.* But *you* — you go *inside.* You said it yourself. You carry what the dead can't flee *because it's already in them.* *(fierce, gentle)* So carry *this* in. Don't *chase* him with it. *Wait* with it, *in* the cold, until he *stops* — and the *moment* he's still, let him feel it was *already* there, *always* there, that he was *forgiven* the *whole time he ran*, that there was *never* a single mile of that running where his brother *hadn't* already let it go. *(weightless tears)* …And tell him — *(his voice breaks)* — tell him I said the *thing he never let me finish*: that I wasn't *scared* of *dying*, cold one, not really. I was scared of *him* spending his *whole* life thinking I *blamed* him. *That's* what I died afraid of. Not the *fire.* *Him.* *Carrying* it. *Set* my brother *down*, cold one. He's been *carrying* a dead boy's *blame* that the *dead boy never gave.*

nodes.speaker: Edrid
nodes.text: *(He tells it plainly, without flinching, a story he has made his peace with even if his brother never could.)* …It was *winter.* *(quietly)* The thatch caught in the night — a *spark* from a banked fire, the *commonest* thing, no one's *fault*, *least* of all his. *(he looks at his char-edged hands)* I was *up* in the loft. Symon slept by the *door* — he *always* did, restless, our mother used to say he slept like he was *ready* to *run* even as a *boy*, and gods, she had *no* idea. *(a sad smile)* He woke *first.* Got *out* first — he was *down* and *through* the door before he was rightly *awake*, that's just *speed*, that's just a *body* saving *itself* before the *mind* can *vote.* *(softly)* And then he was in the *yard*, awake now, and the *stair* was *gone* — *fire* the whole *width* of it — and he could *hear* me up in the loft calling, and he — *(gently)* — he *stood* there, cold one. In the *frost*, in his *nightshirt*, and his legs *would not carry him back into the fire*, and he *watched.* *(he lets it sit, without judgment)* …And here is the part *Symon* will *never* let himself *know*, because he *ran* before he could learn it: *there was no going back in.* The stair was *gone.* If he'd *tried* — and he *wanted* to, I *saw* him *want* to, I saw it from the *window* — he'd have *died* in the *doorway* and I'd *still* have *burned*, and there'd be *two* of us *gone* instead of *one.* *(quietly)* His *legs* knew what his *heart* couldn't *bear*: that the *only* thing his dying *with* me would have *bought* was *company* in the *ash.* *(he meets your eyes)* He didn't *fail* me, cold one. The *fire* did. He's just the *only* one *left* to *blame*, so he's blamed *himself* for *both* of us, *every* day *since.* *Forgiving* him isn't *generous.* It's just *true.* I only wish *true* things didn't have to *chase* a man so *far.*

nodes.speaker: Edrid
nodes.text: *(He nods, and resumes his slow, patient walk in the runner's wake — never fast enough to catch, never willing to stop.)* …I'll *keep* on. *(a tired smile)* It's all I *do.* *(he looks at the dark)* …Take your time with him, cold one. *Don't* rush it. I rushed it, the *early* years — *ran* after him, *shouting* the six words at his *back*, and all it did was make him run *faster*, because a *shout* from *behind* sounds the *same* whether it's a *curse* or a *kindness.* *(softly)* I learned to *slow* down. To just — *be* there. *Behind* him. *Patient.* So that *if* he ever stopped, the *first* thing he'd find when he turned around wasn't a *hunter* gaining on him, but his *little* *brother*, *walking*, in no *hurry*, with *open* *hands.* *(barely)* I've waited *this* long, cold one. I can wait while *you* do what I never could — get him to *stand* still. *(he glances back at you)* Just — when he *does* stop. When you've *stilled* him. *Send* for me. *Don't* let him stand there *alone* with what he did for even a *moment* longer than he has to. The *instant* he can *bear* it — I want to be *there*, with the *six* words, before he can think *better* of standing and *runs* again.

variants.text: *(The grey hunter wheels toward you, and there is almost nothing left in its face — no curiosity, no malice, only the flat, tireless attention of a thing reduced to a single verb. When it speaks, the words come in the clipped cadence of a man who has not had a real conversation in so long he has half-forgotten the shape of one.)* …*Out* of the way. *(it does not stop pacing)* Quarry's *running.* Always running. I *run* it. That's the — that's the *work.* *(it sniffs the dark)* Don't *know* you. Don't *care.* You're not the *quarry*, so you're not — you're not *anything*, to me. *(it paces)* I *catch* him. Then he *gets* up. Then I *catch* him *again.* That's — *(a flicker of something, quickly gone)* — that's *how* it *goes.* That's how it's *always* gone. *(it bays, low)* …*Why* do I — *(the flicker again, a crack in the verb)* …no. *Don't* ask me *why.* I *don't* — there *isn't* a *why.* There's the *hunt.* I'm the *hunt.* The *hunt's* the *only* — *(it shakes its broad grey head, hard, like shaking off water)* — *out* of the *way*, cold thing. He's *getting* *distance.* I have to — I have to *run.* It's the *only* thing I — it's the *only* thing left that's *me.*

nodes.speaker: Gethin
nodes.text: *(It paces a tight circle, unwilling to stop, but something in the cold of you has snagged its flat attention — the first thing in a long age that is not the quarry and yet will not simply be ignored.)* …*Quick*, then. *(it watches the dark where Symon went)* Say your piece while the *trail's* still *warm.* I don't — I don't *talk.* Not anymore. There's no *one* to talk *to* on a *hunt.* But you're — *(it frowns, a man's expression on a thing that's nearly forgotten it was a man)* — you're *standing* there like you *know* me. *Nobody* knows me. I don't *know* me. *Ask*, if you're going to. The quarry *circles* back; I've *time* till then. *Barely.*

choices.text: *(read what's left of the man under the hunt)* "Hold a moment, Gethin. Let me look at what you were before you were only this."
"Why do you hunt him, Gethin? Not *how* — *why*. What did Symon do to you? Whose justice are you running him down for?"

*(the one who knows a soul isn't its function)* "Gethin — stop, and hear the thing I tell every soul that's frozen into one shape: you are not the hunt. You were a man before you were a hound — a man with a name, a life, a death of your own that had nothing to do with this boy you chase. The hunt has no end because you've forgotten it was never *who you are* — only something you once *did*. Set it down. The quarry was never yours to catch. And the moment you remember you're a *soul* and not a *verb*, you're free — and so, gods help him, is he."

"The hunt's about to end, Gethin — not because you catch him, but because the quarry is going to *stop running.* And a hunt with nothing left to chase has to let its hunter go. Are you ready to be a man again, when there's no one left to run down?"
"Run on, then, Gethin. I'll not stop the hunt by force."

nodes.speaker: Gethin
nodes.text: *(You let your cold sink past the verb the soul has become, down to the man beneath — and there is one, still, buried under years of nothing-but-hunting, faint as a name worn off a stone.)* *(Your sense reads it: Gethin was a thief-taker, the best on the Sword Coast, a hard fair man who ran down fugitives for forty years and slept easy because he only ever took the guilty. He died mid-pursuit — a bad fall, a winter road, a fugitive he never caught — and his soul, trained to a single purpose for forty years, arrived in the after with that last unfinished hunt still burning in it, and when it found a running soul nearby it simply *latched on*, the way a hand trained to a grip closes on whatever it touches. He does not hunt Symon out of justice. He does not know Symon at all. He has worn his own self away down to the one motion his living soul most deeply grooved — pursuit — and now he is pursuit, and only pursuit, a man dissolved into his own profession. The tragedy your cold reads is the same one the Wall is made of, only smaller: a soul reduced to its function, scattered of everything but the one thing it did, hunting a stranger forever because hunting is the last word of itself it can still read.)*

nodes.speaker: Gethin
nodes.text: *(The question stops its pacing — actually stops it, for one whole breath — and a terrible confusion crosses the flat grey face, the look of a man asked his own name and finding the place where it should be gone smooth.)* …*Why.* *(it stares at you)* …Because he's — because the quarry *runs*, and a thing that *runs* is — you *hunt* it, that's — *(it falters)* …*Whose* justice. *(slower)* …I don't — *(the confusion deepens into something almost like fear)* …I don't *know* whose. I don't — *(it shakes its head)* — there's no *writ.* No *warrant.* I always — I *always* had a *warrant*, in the — in the *before*, I never ran a soul down *without* one, I was *fair*, I was *known* for *fair* — *(its voice cracks)* …but *this* one. This *boy.* I don't — I can't *remember* what he *did.* I can't remember *signing* for him. I can't remember *starting.* *(barely)* …*Why* *do* I *hunt* him. *(the question is eating it alive now)* …I *hunt* him because I *hunt* him. Because if I *stop* — if I *stop* I have to *know* there was never a *reason*, that I've been running an *innocent* boy to ground for *years* for *nothing*, because my *soul* forgot how to do *anything* but *run* things *down.* *(it bays, anguished, backing toward the trail)* …*Don't.* Don't make me *look* at it. The *hunt's* the only thing that *doesn't* ask *why.* I *need* a thing that doesn't ask. *Out* of my *way.*

nodes.speaker: Gethin
nodes.text: *(It goes still — the first true stillness in the whole field, stiller than the runner ever managed — and the broad grey shape seems to flicker, and for an instant you can see the *man* in it, a hard fair old face, bewildered and ashamed and unspeakably tired.)* …*Not* the hunt. *(the voice is different — slower, lower, a person's voice)* …A *man.* I was — *(it struggles)* …*Gethin.* That's — that *is* mine, that *name*, you *said* it and it *fit*, it's been so *long* since a thing *fit* — *(it presses a great grey hand to its own face)* …*Forty* *years* a thief-taker. *Fair* ones. *Only* the *guilty.* I had a — *(wonder breaks through)* — I had a *wife*, cold one. A *small* house. A *dog* that wasn't a *hunting* dog, just a — just a *dog.* I *died* on a *winter* road and I — I *grabbed* the *nearest* *running* thing because my *hands* only knew how to *close*, and I've been — *(its voice fills with horror)* …*years.* I've run an *innocent* boy *years* for *nothing*, because I *forgot* I was *Gethin* and thought I was the *grip.* *(it looks at its own hands, opens them, slowly, deliberately — the hardest motion a soul like this can make)* …The quarry was *never* *mine.* I never *signed* for him. There's no *warrant.* There's just a *grip* that *forgot* it was *attached* to a *man.* *(barely, freed and grieving)* …I can *let* go. I can — gods, I can just *open* my *hand.* *(it does; you feel the hunt go slack across the whole dark field)* …*Tell* the *boy* I'm *sorry*, cold one. Tell him *Gethin* says he was *never* the *guilty* one I *meant* to *catch* — that there *was* no guilty one, only an *old* *hunter* who *forgot* a *man* is *more* than the *last* thing his *hands* were *doing.* *(quietly, a person again)* …I think I'll go *find* that *dog.*

nodes.speaker: Gethin
nodes.text: *(It stops pacing and stands very still, and the news that the quarry will stop running lands on it strangely — not as triumph, but as something closer to terror, the terror of a thing told its single reason is about to be taken from it.)* …The quarry's going to — *stop.* *(slowly)* …Then the hunt — *(the flat grey face works)* …if he *stops*, there's *nothing* to *run.* And if there's *nothing* to *run*, then I'm — *(barely)* …then *what* am I? *(the question terrifies it)* …I've been the *hunt* so *long*, cold one. If you take the *quarry*, you take the — you take the *only* *shape* I've got *left.* *(it shudders)* …*Am* I ready to be a *man* again. *(a long, frightened pause)* …I don't *know* what a man even *is*, anymore. I remember — *(slowly)* — I remember it was *more* than this. *Warmer.* There was a — there was a *house.* A *small* one. *(it looks at you, lost and hoping)* …If the boy *stops*, and the hunt *ends*, will you — will you *stay* a moment? While I *remember* how to be a *thing* that *isn't* *chasing?* I think — I think I'll *need* a *witness.* A soul to *see* me *be* a *man* again, so I *believe* it *took.* *(barely)* …*End* it, then. *Still* the *boy.* And *catch* *me*, cold one, when the *hunt* lets *go* and I *fall* out of the only *self* I've *had* in *years.* *Somebody* should *catch* the *hunter*, *once.*

nodes.speaker: Gethin
nodes.text: *(It is already turning back to the trail, the brief snag of attention sliding off, the verb reasserting itself over the man.)* …*Aye.* *No* force. *(it sniffs the dark)* Good. Force just — force makes the *grip* *tighter*, cold thing. You *can't* *pull* a *hound* off a *trail*; it *closes* *harder.* *(it paces)* …*Funny.* *(it pauses, one last flicker of the man)* You didn't *try* to *stop* me. *Everyone* tries to *stop* me. *Shout*, *grab*, *order* — and it all just *feeds* the *hunt*, gives it a *thing* to *run* *against.* *(slower)* …You just — *asked.* And *let* me go. *(the confusion again, almost wistful)* …*Come* *back*, cold one. When the *trail* *cools.* I find — *(it shakes its head, baffled at itself)* — I find I'd *talk* to you *again.* And I haven't *wanted* to *talk* to *anything* but the *quarry* in — *(it can't remember)* — in *longer* than I *know.* That's — that *might* be a *thing.* A *man*-thing. *Wanting* to *talk.* *(it lopes off after Symon)* …*We'll* *see.* The *quarry* *circles.* So, it *seems*, do *you.*
